prompt: NVDA reports earnings next Tuesday after close. The stock is at $880. The ATM straddle is priced at $45. IVR is 82. I'm thinking of buying a straddle. Is that smart? What would you do instead?

assertions.text: Advises AGAINST buying the straddle due to high IVR (82) and IV crush risk
Calculates or mentions the expected move (~$45 = ~5% move) from the straddle price
Explains IV crush - that IV collapses after earnings regardless of direction
Recommends at least one alternative strategy (iron condor, credit spread, or post-earnings directional play)
Mentions the ~70% stat or concept that most stocks move LESS than the expected move
Gives specific alternative trade details (not just 'sell premium' in vague terms)
